summary: DHH purchased Apple's 23-inch LCD monitor after a price drop and is thrilled by the screen real estate it provides. He describes fitting a browser, TextMate editor, terminal, and database tool all on a single desktop. Despite a minor dead pixel issue, he considers it a productivity boon. He notes that fellow Rails developer Tobias Luetke made the same purchase, and mentions favorable tax depreciation rules in Denmark.
key_points: Apple's price reduction of roughly 25% tipped the purchasing decision
The large display enables a multi-window workflow (browser, editor, terminal, database) on a single desktop
A dead pixel is acknowledged but worked around pragmatically by repositioning windows
The monitor qualified for instant depreciation for Danish companies, especially with a student discount
money_quotes: I can fit a browser, TextMate, a terminal tailing the log, and CocoaMySQL on a single desktop! That's just productivity screaming out right there.
And oh my. Oh my, indeed. It's freaking huge. And gorgeous. But mostly huge.
key_insight: A larger display that fits an entire development workflow on one screen is a straightforward productivity multiplier worth investing in.
